name: chokepoint-lsass-dumping
bodyMarkdown:
# LSASS Credential Dumping — Detection Chokepoint

**Priority:** CRITICAL | **Tactic:** Credential Access | **Difficulty:** MEDIUM | **Prevalence:** VERY HIGH
**Techniques:** T1003.001, T1003, T1547.005

## Overview
To extract credentials from a live Windows system, attacker must read lsass.exe memory via kernel-mediated handle. The handle request fires regardless of tool used — direct syscalls still traverse ObRegisterCallbacks.

## Chokepoint Stages

### 1. Handle Acquisition
Process must request handle to lsass.exe with memory-read rights.
Observable: Sysmon EID 10 (ProcessAccess) with TargetImage=lsass.exe, GrantedAccess field.

### 2. Memory Read
Must read lsass virtual memory via NtReadVirtualMemory or MiniDumpWriteDump.
Observable: EID 10 CallTrace (dbgcore.dll/dbghelp.dll = MiniDump; UNKNOWN = direct syscalls).

### 3. Credential Extraction
Must parse lsass memory structures. Observable: EID 10 (GrantedAccess + CallTrace), EID 7 (SSP DLL injection), EID 11 (dump file on disk).

## Variations (24 tracked)
Mimikatz (0x1010), comsvcs.dll MiniDump, ProcDump, Nanodump (0x0810/UNKNOWN), HandleKatz (0x0040), PPLBlade/PPLdump, Task Manager Dump, SSP Injection, Direct Syscalls (SafetyKatz, Dumpert), Pypykatz, Impacket secretsdump, CrackMapExec/NetExec, Cobalt Strike, Sliver, Havoc, Brute Ratel, Mythic, SharpKatz/SharpDump, Out-Minidump, LSASS Shtinkering, Skeleton Key, LaZagne, EDRSandblast.

## Detection Logic
- **Research:** All non-system processes accessing lsass.exe (EID 10)
- **Hunt:** LSASS access with suspicious CallTrace (dbgcore/dbghelp/UNKNOWN) or non-standard source path
- **Analyst:** Non-standard process + dump mechanism + credential-dump access rights = high-confidence dump

## Prevention
- Enable Windows Credential Guard (VBS)
- Enable LSASS PPL
- Disable WDigest; enforce Kerberos only
